diagrams.title: Single Parity Check (Even Parity)
diagrams.description: Shows how a parity bit is appended to a 7-bit data word to make the total number of 1s even. Visualizes the sender adding the parity bit and the receiver checking for errors by counting 1s.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the simplest form of error detection using parity bits, demonstrating how single-bit errors are detected but not corrected.

diagrams.title: Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) Process
diagrams.description: Visualizes the polynomial division process used in CRC. Shows the dataword being divided by a generator polynomial, the remainder becoming the CRC code, and the receiver performing the same division to detect errors.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how CRC provides more robust error detection than simple parity, showing the mathematical division process and why it's effective for burst errors.

diagrams.title: Hamming Code Error Correction
diagrams.description: Shows the positioning of data bits and parity bits (at positions 1, 2, 4, 8...) in a Hamming code word. Visualizes how parity bits cover different bit positions and how the syndrome bits identify the exact error location for single-bit correction.
diagrams.purpose: Explains both error detection and correction capability of Hamming codes, demonstrating the concept of code distance and how multiple parity checks pinpoint error locations.
